description:
You are the site administrator of a busy web service.  
  For a quick performance-oriented audit you need a **single, efficient shell command** (you may chain pipes, but must launch only one command line) that produces a compact summary of “active” user accounts extracted from the text database stored at  

        /home/user/data/users_db/passwd.txt  

  Definition of an “active” user for this report:  
    • UID is 1000 or higher (field 3 in the passwd format).  
    • The login shell (field 7) is exactly “/bin/bash”.  

  The command must generate the file  

        /home/user/account_summary.log  

  in **exactly** the following format so that an automated test can verify it verbatim:  

        User_Count: <number_of_active_users>
        <username1>
        <username2>
        ...
        <usernameN>

  where  
    • “<number_of_active_users>” is the total count of qualifying users.  
    • Each subsequent line lists a qualifying username (field 1) in **alphabetical order, one per line**.  
    • No extra blank lines or spaces should be present anywhere in the file.  
    • The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e. the last username line is immediately followed by “\n”).  

  Constraints & hints:  
    • Use a single command line; avoid iterative loops or temporary files for performance.  
    • You **may** combine multiple utilities with pipes inside that one line.  
    • Do not use sudo or attempt to modify /etc/passwd; work only with the provided text file.  
    • When you are confident that the summary is correct, display the contents of /home/user/account_summary.log to confirm completion.
truth:
Initial files and directories present before the task starts:
    • Directory: /home/user/data/users_db
    • File: /home/user/data/users_db/passwd.txt

  Exact contents of /home/user/data/users_db/passwd.txt (7 colon-separated fields per traditional /etc/passwd layout):

        root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
        daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/usr/sbin:/usr/sbin/nologin
        alice:x:1000:1000:Alice Admin:/home/alice:/bin/bash
        bob:x:1001:1001:Bob Builder:/home/bob:/bin/zsh
        charlie:x:1002:1002:Charlie Chaplin:/home/charlie:/bin/bash
        dave:x:1003:1003:Dave Developer:/home/dave:/usr/sbin/nologin
        erin:x:1004:1004:Erin Example:/home/erin:/bin/bash

  Active users that satisfy both conditions (UID ≥ 1000 and shell == /bin/bash):
        alice
        charlie
        erin

  Therefore, after the agent completes the task correctly, the file /home/user/account_summary.log MUST exist with the exact contents (including the final newline):

        User_Count: 3
        alice
        charlie
        erin

  File permissions: the container run as the “user” account already has write permission inside /home/user; no root privileges are needed.

  The automated test will:
    1. Verify that /home/user/account_summary.log exists.
    2. Read the file and assert its byte-exact contents match the block above.
name: task_000000_dcdac779
